citations.narrative:
Water receptacles are continuing to not being kept clean and sanitary. Approximately seven water bottles that provide
water access for seven individual chinchillas had a green substance on the interior of the bottles. The water also
appeared cloudy in color. Upon further inspection when the bottles were disturbed there was green/brown debris floating
throughout water. The licensee stated during the inspection that these water receptacles had not been cleaned since the
previous inspection on 21-January-2026. A lack of access to potable water can lead to waterborne diseases, dehydration,
or death. Correct by ensuring that all animals are provided water as often as necessary for the health and comfort of the
animal. Frequency of watering shall consider age, species, condition, size and type of animal.

There is an insufficient number of adequately trained employees to carry out the level of husbandry practices and care
required by the Animal Welfare Act. There is only one full-time person responsible for the care of approximately 363
animals at this facility. The facility did not correct the direct non-compliant item on the previous inspection report dated
1/21/2026 regarding insanitary water receptacles. Additionally, there is a history of numerous non-compliances identified
pertaining to the cleaning, sanitation, watering, and veterinary care.
The issues seen are consistent with shortages of personnel. Insufficient personnel can lead to a failure in maintenance of
the facility, delayed care for the animals, and unnecessary suffering and health concerns for the animals. The facility must
ensure a sufficient number of adequately trained employees are utilized to maintain the professionally acceptable level of
husbandry practices set forth in this subpart. Such practices shall be under the supervision of an animal caretaker who
has a background in animal husbandry or care. Correct by 5-February-2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the Facility Representative.
Additional Inspectors:
Jonathan Tomkovitch, VETERINARY MEDICAL OFFICER n

narrative:
3.130  Direct Repeat
   
Watering. 
Water receptacles are continuing to not being kept clean and sanitary. Approximately seven water bottles that provide 
water access for seven individual chinchillas had a green substance on the interior of the bottles. The water also 
appeared cloudy in color. Upon further inspection when the bottles were disturbed there was green/brown debris floating 
throughout water. The licensee stated during the inspection that these water receptacles had not been cleaned since the 
previous inspection on 21-January-2026. A lack of access to potable water can lead to waterborne diseases, dehydration, 
or death. Correct by ensuring that all animals are provided water as often as necessary for the health and comfort of the 
animal. Frequency of watering shall consider age, species, condition, size and type of animal. 
3.132  Direct
   
Employees. 
There is an insufficient number of adequately trained employees to carry out the level of husbandry practices and care 
required by the Animal Welfare Act. There is only one full-time person responsible for the care of approximately 363 
animals at this facility. The facility did not correct the direct non-compliant item on the previous inspection report dated 
1/21/2026 regarding insanitary water receptacles. Additionally, there is a history of numerous non-compliances identified 
pertaining to the cleaning, sanitation, watering, and veterinary care. 
 
The issues seen are consistent with shortages of personnel. Insufficient personnel can lead to a failure in maintenance of 
the facility, delayed care for the animals, and unnecessary suffering and health concerns for the animals. The facility must 
ensure a sufficient number of adequately trained employees are utilized to maintain the professionally acceptable level of 
husbandry practices set forth in this subpart. Such practices shall be under the supervision of an animal caretaker who 
has a background in animal husbandry or care. Correct by 5-February-2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the Facility Representative. 
 
Additional Inspectors: 
Jonathan Tomkovitch, VETERINARY MEDICAL OFFICER n
